info.title: Klaviyo API (Beta)
info.description: The Klaviyo REST API. Please visit https://developers.klaviyo.com for more details.

post.description:
> 🚧 This endpoint is in beta and subject to change. > > A beta revision header (2026-04-15.pre) is required to use our beta APIs. Klaviyo APIs in beta are not intended for use in production. See our [versioning and deprecation policy](https://developers.klaviyo.com/en/docs/api_versioning_and_deprecation_policy) for more information. Adds a new external HTTP tool Customer Agent skills can call. Provide protocol details (method, URL, query parameter, header, and body templates using Jinja-style ``{{variable_name}}`` syntax) and declare the variables those templates reference. The runtime uses ``variables`` to determine which values are supplied at call time. To reference a secret, include a variable with ``source: secret`` and ``value`` set to the ``agent- secret`` id. The tool isn't directly callable until bound to a skill with ``PATCH /agent-skills/{id}``.

*Rate limits*:
Burst: `10/s`
Steady: `150/m` **Scopes:** `agents:write`

requestBody.description:
Request to create an external HTTP tool Customer Agent skills can
call.

get.description:
> 🚧 This endpoint is in beta and subject to change. > > A beta revision header (2026-04-15.pre) is required to use our beta APIs. Klaviyo APIs in beta are not intended for use in production. See our [versioning and deprecation policy](https://developers.klaviyo.com/en/docs/api_versioning_and_deprecation_policy) for more information. Returns every external tool (HTTP API endpoint) Customer Agent skills can call. Each tool has an id, display name, protocol details (method, URL template), authentication setup, and any referenced secrets. Use this before creating a new tool to avoid duplicates, or before binding a tool to a skill to confirm the tool exists.

*Rate limits*:
Burst: `10/s`
Steady: `150/m` **Scopes:** `agents:read`

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
For more information please visit https://developers.klaviyo.com/en/v2026-04-15.pre/reference/api-overview#filtering
Allowed field(s)/operator(s):
`name`: `contains`
`tool_type`: `equals`
`created_at`: `equals`, `greater-or-equal`, `less-or-equal`
`updated_at`: `equals`, `greater-or-equal`, `less-or-equal`

get.description:
> 🚧 This endpoint is in beta and subject to change. > > A beta revision header (2026-04-15.pre) is required to use our beta APIs. Klaviyo APIs in beta are not intended for use in production. See our [versioning and deprecation policy](https://developers.klaviyo.com/en/docs/api_versioning_and_deprecation_policy) for more information. Returns full configuration for a single tool: protocol, auth method, templated request configuration, variables, and referenced secrets. Use to inspect a tool's setup before referencing it from a skill or modifying its config.

*Rate limits*:
Burst: `10/s`
Steady: `150/m` **Scopes:** `agents:read`

patch.description:
> 🚧 This endpoint is in beta and subject to change. > > A beta revision header (2026-04-15.pre) is required to use our beta APIs. Klaviyo APIs in beta are not intended for use in production. See our [versioning and deprecation policy](https://developers.klaviyo.com/en/docs/api_versioning_and_deprecation_policy) for more information. Patches the tool's config in place: protocol, templated request configuration, variables, auth, and referenced secrets. All skills bound to this tool pick up the new behavior immediately on next call.

*Rate limits*:
Burst: `10/s`
Steady: `150/m` **Scopes:** `agents:write`

requestBody.description: Sparse request to update an external HTTP tool.

delete.description:
> 🚧 This endpoint is in beta and subject to change. > > A beta revision header (2026-04-15.pre) is required to use our beta APIs. Klaviyo APIs in beta are not intended for use in production. See our [versioning and deprecation policy](https://developers.klaviyo.com/en/docs/api_versioning_and_deprecation_policy) for more information. Permanently removes the tool. Skills that referenced it lose the binding; Customer Agent will not be able to use that tool with those skills until it is rebound.

*Rate limits*:
Burst: `10/s`
Steady: `150/m` **Scopes:** `agents:write`

get.summary: Get Skill IDs for Agent Tool

get.summary: Get Secret IDs for Agent Tool
get.description:
> 🚧 This endpoint is in beta and subject to change. > > A beta revision header (2026-04-15.pre) is required to use our beta APIs. Klaviyo APIs in beta are not intended for use in production. See our [versioning and deprecation policy](https://developers.klaviyo.com/en/docs/api_versioning_and_deprecation_policy) for more information. List Agent Secret IDs referenced by an Agent Tool.

*Rate limits*:
Burst: `10/s`
Steady: `150/m` **Scopes:** `agents:read`

display_name.description: Human-readable name shown for the skill.

name.description: Skill name from the backing system.

description.description: Summary of the skill's capabilities and when Customer Agent should use it.

instructions.description: System prompt the skill uses to handle a turn.

created_at.description: Date and time when the skill was created.

updated_at.description: Date and time when the skill was last updated.

name.description: Machine key used in instruction tokens and merged into raw input.

label.description: End-user facing label shown in the configuration UI.

description.description: Optional help text rendered alongside the UI control.

input_type.description: UI control type. Values are persisted as list[str].

required.description: Whether the configuration parameter is required.

name.description: Human-facing display name for the tool.

public_description.description: Public description of the tool and when Customer Agent should use it.

type.description: Create detail variant for custom HTTPS tools.

protocol.description: Message protocol for the tool. `https` is currently supported.

items.description: Template variables referenced by the URL, query parameters, headers, or body. To reference a secret, include a variable with `source: secret` and `value` set to the `agent-secret` id.

timeout_seconds.description: Overall tool execution timeout in seconds. Must be greater than 0 and at most 10.

max_retries.description: Maximum retry attempts for the tool call. Must be between 0 and 3.

protocol.description: Message protocol for this custom tool.

items.description: Template variables available to the request template.

timeout_seconds.description: Overall tool execution timeout in seconds.

max_retries.description: Maximum retry attempts for the tool call.

created_at.description: Date and time when the tool was created, if available.

updated_at.description: Date and time when the tool was last updated, if available.

type.description: Read detail variant for custom HTTPS tools.
Update detail variant for custom HTTPS tools.

protocol.description: Message protocol for the tool. `https` is currently supported.

request_template.description: Sparse update for the templated outbound HTTPS request configuration.

items.description: Replacement list of template variables referenced by the URL, query parameters, headers, or body.

timeout_seconds.description: Overall tool execution timeout in seconds. Must be greater than 0 and at most 10.

max_retries.description: Maximum retry attempts for the tool call. Must be between 0 and 3.

url_template.description: Outbound URL template. Use Jinja-style variable interpolation, for example `https://example.com/orders/{{order_id}}`.

http_method.description: HTTP method for the outbound request.

request_timeout_seconds.description: Outbound request timeout in seconds. Must be greater than 0 and at most 10.

items.description: Outbound query parameters. Values may use Jinja-style variables such as `{{email}}`.
Outbound HTTP headers. Values may use Jinja-style variables such as `{{api_key}}`; for secrets, declare a variable with `source: secret` and reference it by name in the template.

body_template.description: JSON request body template. String values may use Jinja-style variables such as `{{order_id}}`.

url_template.description: Outbound URL template. Use Jinja-style variable interpolation, for example `https://example.com/orders/{{order_id}}`.

http_method.description: HTTP method for the outbound request.

request_timeout_seconds.description: Outbound request timeout in seconds. Must be greater than 0 and at most 10.

items.description: Outbound query parameters. Values may use Jinja-style variables such as `{{email}}`.
Outbound HTTP headers. Values may use Jinja-style variables such as `{{api_key}}`; for secrets, declare a variable with `source: secret` and reference it by name in the template.

body_template.description: JSON request body template. String values may use Jinja-style variables such as `{{order_id}}`.

items.description: Author-time configuration parameters supported by this tool.

type.description: Read detail variant for integration-backed tools.

items.description: Author-time configuration parameters supported by this tool.

type.description: Read detail variant for first-party Klaviyo tools.

value.description: Parameter value, optionally using Jinja-style variables.

name.description: Human-facing display name for the tool.

public_description.description: Public description of the tool and when Customer Agent should use it.

details.description: Protocol-specific configuration and read details for the tool.

public_name.description: Optional customer-facing name for the tool.

name.description: Human-facing display name for the tool.

public_description.description: Public description of the tool and when Customer Agent should use it.

details.description: Sparse custom HTTPS tool configuration details.

id.description: Agent Tool identifier. This must match the `tool_id` path parameter.

required.description: Whether the variable is required when the tool is called.

description.description: Optional description of the variable for agent/tool-call context.

source.description: Where the variable value is resolved from. `dynamic` values are supplied at tool-call time. `klaviyo` values reference a Klaviyo-provided value with `value` set to that reference id. `secret` values reference an `agent-secret` with `value` set to the secret id.

value.description: Reference value for non-dynamic variables. For `source: secret`, this is the `agent-secret` id. For `source: klaviyo`, this is the Klaviyo reference id. For `source: dynamic`, omit this field or set it to null.

Klaviyo-API-Key.name: Authorization
Klaviyo-API-Key.description:
Private key authentication for /api/ endpoints is performed by setting the `Authorization` header to `Klaviyo-API-Key your-private-api-key`
For more information please visit https://developers.klaviyo.com/en/v2026-04-15.pre/reference/api-overview#authentication
